explorationLog.title: Two Student Pathways into Design
explorationLog.details: Students fall into two categories: Early Fascination (knew they wanted design early) and Late Discovery (transferred or found later). Need messaging supporting both.

explorationLog.title: Reframing "Design Excellence"
explorationLog.details: Excellence shouldn't be defined just by comparing to SCAD or RISD, but by interdisciplinary mobility, career outcomes, and intellectual curiosity.

explorationLog.title: Visual Evidence of Hands-on Experience?
explorationLog.details: Alumni stories were explicitly requested by students, wanting to see design in action. How do we produce this cohesively?

tasks.task: Upload comprehensive research slides from focus groups
Develop cohesive CTA language for design stories

recommendations.title: Focus Differentiators on Flexibility over Refinement
recommendations.why: Kent State fosters adaptability and shifting disciplines unlike institutions tracking students directly into narrow artistic lanes.

recommendations.title: Highlight Early UXD and Embedded Engineering
recommendations.why: These applied disciplines distinctly elevate our design narrative and match modern career outcome demands.

themeNotes:
# Design Theme Core Tenets

- Emphasize interdisciplinary collaboration across eight design disciplines.
- Build narratives that connect to career outcome alignment (UXD, systems design).
- Primary audience: Prospective students & parents. Secondary: Exploratory undeclared students.

meetingNotes.title: Design Excellence Strategy Meeting
meetingNotes.summary: Centered on redefining Design Excellence via focus groups. Discussed the two pathways into design, prioritizing versatility over artistic refinement, and identified gaps where alumni stories are needed to show design in action.

competitors: SCAD (Savannah College of Art and Design)
